title: Schefflera lancifoliolata
taxonomy: Schefflera lancifoliolata is classified within the family Araliaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as ginsengs, ivies, and ivy beans. This classification places it within the order Apiales. Schefflera lancifoliolata is further categorized as a member of the genus Schefflera, which comprises over 600 species of evergreen shrubs and small trees. The genus Schefflera is native to tropical regions of the world, including Central and South America, Africa, and Southeast Asia. Schefflera lancifoliolata specifically is native to New Caledonia.
uses: Schefflera lancifoliolata has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Traditionally, it is believed to have antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also used to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. However, its medicinal uses require further research for verification. More studies are needed to fully understand the plant's potential in modern medicine.

intro: Schefflera lancifoliolata, also known as the yellow torch plant, is a flowering plant species in the family Araliaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Schefflera lancifoliolata include lignans, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Schefflera lancifoliolata is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
